pairs.distinction: Authority is recognized directive standing; power is operative capacity to produce outcomes.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat recognized directive standing and operative outcome capacity as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between authority and power is not admitted.
conflictCauses: An authority claim assigns identityKind operative_outcome_capacity.
A power claim assigns identityKind recognized_directive_standing.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between authority and power.

pairs.distinction: Authority identifies who may direct; legitimacy evaluates whether rule or standing counts as valid.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat recognized directive standing and accepted validity as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between authority and legitimacy is not admitted.
conflictCauses: An authority claim assigns identityKind accepted_justified_validity.
A legitimacy claim assigns identityKind recognized_directive_standing.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between authority and legitimacy.

pairs.distinction: Power is operative capacity; legitimacy is accepted or justified validity.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat operative outcome capacity and accepted validity as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between power and legitimacy is not admitted.
conflictCauses: A power claim assigns identityKind accepted_justified_validity.
A legitimacy claim assigns identityKind operative_outcome_capacity.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between power and legitimacy.
